model_narrative: This is a nurse rostering problem. We decide which nurses to assign to which working shifts, where each working shift is a combination of a shift slot, a department, and a day. From those assignments we track how many hours each nurse works, how evenly the work is spread across nurses, the labor cost run up by each department, and the total number of assignments made. The objective is to minimize a composite of the total departmental cost, a penalty on unfair workload spread, and a penalty on the total number of assignments, with the last two scaled by their own weights.

sets.name: nurse
sets.doc: the nurses that can be rostered

sets.name: shift
sets.doc: the named shift slots that can be staffed

sets.name: department
sets.doc: the hospital departments that need staffing

sets.name: skill
sets.doc: the clinical skills a nurse can hold

sets.name: day
sets.doc: the days of the planning horizon

sets.name: nh
sets.doc: the attribute headers describing each nurse

sets.name: sh
sets.doc: the attribute headers describing each shift, namely when it starts and ends and how many nurses it needs at least and at most

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the actual working shifts, each one a combination of a shift slot, a department, and a day that is really run; an assignment decision exists only for these combinations

sets.name: nurseSkills
sets.doc: the pairs telling which nurse holds which skill; the first entry is the nurse and the second is the skill, and a pair is present exactly when that nurse has that skill

sets.name: vacation
sets.doc: the pairs telling which nurse is on vacation on which day; the first entry is the nurse and the second is the day. A nurse cannot be assigned to any shift that falls on a day they are on vacation, and those assignment decisions are held at zero

sets.name: nurseAssoc
sets.doc: the pairs of nurses who are associated and must be rostered together; the first and second entries are the two nurses in the pair

sets.name: nurseIncompat
sets.doc: the pairs of nurses who are incompatible and must not be rostered together; the first and second entries are the two nurses in the pair

params.name: nurseData
params.doc: the nurse attribute table keyed by nurse and attribute; it holds each nurse's seniority, qualification level, and pay rate, where pay rate is the cost per hour worked by that nurse

params.name: shiftData
params.doc: the shift attribute table keyed by shift slot, department, day, and attribute; it holds each shift's start time, end time, minimum nurse requirement, and maximum nurse requirement

params.name: skillRequirements
params.doc: the number of nurses holding a given skill that a department needs on any of its shifts; a value of zero means that department places no requirement on that skill

params.name: startTime
params.doc: the continuous start time of a working shift, expressed on a horizon-wide clock so shifts on later days have larger values and overlap can be compared directly

params.name: endTime
params.doc: the continuous end time of a working shift on the same horizon-wide clock; the length in hours of a shift is its end time minus its start time

params.name: maxWorkTime
params.doc: the maximum number of hours any nurse is allowed to work over the horizon

params.name: fairnessWeight
params.doc: the weight placed on the fairness penalty in the objective

params.name: assignmentWeight
params.doc: the weight placed on the total-assignment penalty in the objective

vars.name: nurseAssignments
vars.doc: one when a nurse is assigned to a working shift and zero otherwise

vars.name: nurseWorkTime
vars.doc: the total hours a nurse works over the horizon, bounded above by the maximum work time

vars.name: nurseAvgHours
vars.doc: the average hours worked across all nurses

vars.name: nurseMoreThanAvgHours
vars.doc: the amount by which a nurse works more than the average, the overtime piece

vars.name: nurseLessThanAvgHours
vars.doc: the amount by which a nurse works less than the average, the undertime piece

vars.name: fairness
vars.doc: the aggregate of all nurses' overtime and undertime, used to penalize uneven workloads

vars.name: costByDepartments
vars.doc: the labor cost incurred by a department

vars.name: totalAssignments
vars.doc: the total number of nurse-to-shift assignments made

description: For each department, work out the labor cost it runs up and set that department's cost equal to it. The cost comes from the nurses actually working the shifts that belong to this department. For every such working shift, a nurse who is assigned to it contributes the length of that shift in hours priced at that nurse's hourly pay rate. Add those contributions up across all nurses and all of the department's working shifts to get the department's cost.

model_narrative: This is a nurse rostering problem. We decide which nurses to assign to which working shifts, where each working shift is a combination of a shift slot, a department, and a day. From those assignments we track how many hours each nurse works, how evenly the work is spread across nurses, the labor cost run up by each department, and the total number of assignments made. The objective is to minimize a composite of the total departmental cost, a penalty on unfair workload spread, and a penalty on the total number of assignments, with the last two scaled by their own weights.

description: Every working shift has to be staffed with at least the number of nurses it requires at a minimum. For each working shift, count how many nurses are assigned to it and make sure that count is no less than that shift's minimum requirement.

description: No working shift may be staffed with more nurses than it is allowed to have. For each working shift, count how many nurses are assigned to it and make sure that count does not exceed that shift's maximum requirement.

description: For each nurse, set the total hours that nurse works equal to the time they spend on the shifts they are assigned to. For every working shift, a nurse who is assigned to it adds the length of that shift in hours. Sum those lengths over all the shifts the nurse takes to get that nurse's total working time.

model_narrative: This is a nurse rostering problem. We decide which nurses to assign to which working shifts, where each working shift is a combination of a shift slot, a department, and a day. From those assignments we track how many hours each nurse works, how evenly the work is spread across nurses, the labor cost run up by each department, and the total number of assignments made. The objective is to minimize a composite of the total departmental cost, a penalty on unfair workload spread, and a penalty on the total number of assignments, with the last two scaled by their own weights.

description: A nurse cannot work two shifts that run at the same time. For any nurse and any two different working shifts that clash in time, allow the nurse to take at most one of the two. Two shifts clash when the second one starts at or after the first one starts and before the first one ends. Apply this only to pairs of shifts that actually overlap in this way, and skip a shift paired with itself.

model_narrative: This is a nurse rostering problem. We decide which nurses to assign to which working shifts, where each working shift is a combination of a shift slot, a department, and a day. From those assignments we track how many hours each nurse works, how evenly the work is spread across nurses, the labor cost run up by each department, and the total number of assignments made. The objective is to minimize a composite of the total departmental cost, a penalty on unfair workload spread, and a penalty on the total number of assignments, with the last two scaled by their own weights.

description: Some pairs of nurses are associated and must always be rostered together. For any such associated pair and any working shift, require that either both nurses are assigned to that shift or neither of them is, so their assignments to that shift always match. Apply this only to the pairs that are actually flagged as associated.

model_narrative: This is a nurse rostering problem. We decide which nurses to assign to which working shifts, where each working shift is a combination of a shift slot, a department, and a day. From those assignments we track how many hours each nurse works, how evenly the work is spread across nurses, the labor cost run up by each department, and the total number of assignments made. The objective is to minimize a composite of the total departmental cost, a penalty on unfair workload spread, and a penalty on the total number of assignments, with the last two scaled by their own weights.

sets.name: nurse
sets.doc: the nurses that can be rostered

sets.name: shift
sets.doc: the named shift slots that can be staffed

sets.name: department
sets.doc: the hospital departments that need staffing

sets.name: skill
sets.doc: the clinical skills a nurse can hold

sets.name: day
sets.doc: the days of the planning horizon

sets.name: nh
sets.doc: the attribute headers describing each nurse

sets.name: sh
sets.doc: the attribute headers describing each shift, namely when it starts and ends and how many nurses it needs at least and at most

sets.name: s
sets.doc: the actual working shifts, each one a combination of a shift slot, a department, and a day that is really run; an assignment decision exists only for these combinations

sets.name: nurseSkills
sets.doc: the pairs telling which nurse holds which skill; the first entry is the nurse and the second is the skill, and a pair is present exactly when that nurse has that skill

sets.name: vacation
sets.doc: the pairs telling which nurse is on vacation on which day; the first entry is the nurse and the second is the day. A nurse cannot be assigned to any shift that falls on a day they are on vacation, and those assignment decisions are held at zero

sets.name: nurseAssoc
sets.doc: the pairs of nurses who are associated and must be rostered together; the first and second entries are the two nurses in the pair

sets.name: nurseIncompat
sets.doc: the pairs of nurses who are incompatible and must not be rostered together; the first and second entries are the two nurses in the pair

params.name: nurseData
params.doc: the nurse attribute table keyed by nurse and attribute; it holds each nurse's seniority, qualification level, and pay rate, where pay rate is the cost per hour worked by that nurse

params.name: shiftData
params.doc: the shift attribute table keyed by shift slot, department, day, and attribute; it holds each shift's start time, end time, minimum nurse requirement, and maximum nurse requirement

params.name: skillRequirements
params.doc: the number of nurses holding a given skill that a department needs on any of its shifts; a value of zero means that department places no requirement on that skill

params.name: startTime
params.doc: the continuous start time of a working shift, expressed on a horizon-wide clock so shifts on later days have larger values and overlap can be compared directly

params.name: endTime
params.doc: the continuous end time of a working shift on the same horizon-wide clock; the length in hours of a shift is its end time minus its start time

params.name: maxWorkTime
params.doc: the maximum number of hours any nurse is allowed to work over the horizon

params.name: fairnessWeight
params.doc: the weight placed on the fairness penalty in the objective

params.name: assignmentWeight
params.doc: the weight placed on the total-assignment penalty in the objective

vars.name: nurseAssignments
vars.doc: one when a nurse is assigned to a working shift and zero otherwise

vars.name: nurseWorkTime
vars.doc: the total hours a nurse works over the horizon, bounded above by the maximum work time

vars.name: nurseAvgHours
vars.doc: the average hours worked across all nurses

vars.name: nurseMoreThanAvgHours
vars.doc: the amount by which a nurse works more than the average, the overtime piece

vars.name: nurseLessThanAvgHours
vars.doc: the amount by which a nurse works less than the average, the undertime piece

vars.name: fairness
vars.doc: the aggregate of all nurses' overtime and undertime, used to penalize uneven workloads

vars.name: costByDepartments
vars.doc: the labor cost incurred by a department

vars.name: totalAssignments
vars.doc: the total number of nurse-to-shift assignments made

description: Each department's skill requirements must be met on every one of its working shifts. When a department requires a certain number of nurses with a particular skill, then on each working shift belonging to that department the number of assigned nurses who hold that skill has to be at least the required number. Apply this only where the shift belongs to the department in question and that department actually places a positive requirement on the skill.

description: Define the average working hours across the nurses. The number of nurses times the average hours must equal the total hours worked summed over all nurses.

description: For each nurse, split that nurse's working time around the average into an overtime piece and an undertime piece. The nurse's total hours must equal the average hours plus how much they are above the average minus how much they are below it.

description: Define the overall fairness measure as the total spread of workloads away from the average. Add up, over all nurses, each nurse's overtime plus their undertime, and set the fairness measure equal to that total.

From those assignments we track how many hours each nurse works, how evenly the work is spread across nurses, the labor cost run up by each department, and the total number of assignments made. The objective is to minimize a composite of the total departmental cost, a penalty on unfair workload spread, and a penalty on the total number of assignments, with the last two scaled by their own weights.

description: Define the total number of assignments made. Count every nurse-to-shift assignment across all nurses and all working shifts, and set the total assignments equal to that count.
